name: Connector Passage
summary: The flexing space between carriages, where the Train is least convinced of itself.

notable_features: The plates of the floor shift against each other in three pieces, two long and one short. The short one is always under your forward foot. The metal flexes more than metal should and the flex has a sound, like a low word held in the back of the throat.
Cold air sluices in through the seams. It is not weather cold. It is the cold of an interior that has had its outside taken away.
Each crossing of this passage takes a different number of steps. The doors do not move. The space between them does.

san_triggers.trigger_text: You crossed this in nine steps last time. It is taking longer.
The cold coming through the seams is the temperature of where the Fog lives, not weather.

skill_check_hooks.action_hint: count your steps across the passage
keep your balance against the sway
feel for the seam in the dark
